outside_pac.label: ALPHA VALLEY BUSINESS AND TECHNOLOGY CONSORTIUM

rollups.note: Four SEPARATE universes, never blended: FEC campaign committees (political money Swalwell controls), the official House Office / MRA (TAXPAYER money for running his government office — a different thing entirely, not reconciled against FEC), the California state Governor campaign (FPPC), and an outside committee not controlled by him.

flags.title: Phantom second leadership PAC corrected
flags.body: The draft dashboard listed two leadership PACs (C00566059 + C01000165). FEC has no committee C01000165. The real entity is a single PAC, Remedy PAC (C00566059), formerly named 'New Energy PAC.' Counting by name split one renamed committee into two. This rebuild keys every figure by committee ID.
flags.evidence: FEC committee lookup C01000165 → no results.

flags.title: Duplicate source file removed
flags.body: Two Schedule B exports for Remedy PAC were byte-identical (same 2,105 rows). Only one is used; the pipeline dedups by sub_id so duplicate files or rows cannot inflate totals.
flags.evidence: md5 match on the two schedule_b CSVs.

flags.title: Outside PAC is not controlled by Swalwell
flags.body: Alpha Valley Business & Technology Consortium (C00528174) is a single-candidate INDEPENDENT-EXPENDITURE committee: it may spend money ABOUT Swalwell but is not controlled BY him. It is shown as its own entity and can be toggled in/out of the universe total. FEC reports schedule_b $0 and schedule_e $10,420 for it.

flags.title: Governor total = own-campaign spend only
flags.body: Per the researcher, money the Governor committee paid OUT to others and all refunds are excluded from the campaign-spend headline. Gross filed $6,258,169; own-campaign spend $5,987,856. Excluded and shown separately: RETURNED CONTRIBUTIONS: $250,113 (47 rows); CONTRIBUTION: $20,200 (5 rows).
flags.evidence: FPPC expenditure codes CONTRIBUTION + RETURNED CONTRIBUTIONS.
